commit.message:
Simplify waveform: raw buffer + mean subtraction, no online filters

Previous DC-removal filter had float precision issues (accumulator grew
to millions) and int16_t overflow, causing broken display on cheap AEAK
sensors. The 0x7F LED power may also have been too high.

New approach — zero filter state, impossible to break:
- Store raw uint32_t IR values in a 128-sample circular buffer
- At draw time compute the buffer mean (uint64 sum / count) as the
  DC baseline estimate
- Subtract mean from each sample to get AC, then invert (PPG signal
  drops on heartbeat, so inverting gives upward peaks)
- Per-frame min/max auto-scaling of the inverted AC fills the display
- sampleAverage bumped from 4→8 for cleaner signal on cheap sensors
- LED power restored to 0x1F to avoid photodiode saturation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
PR: Fix balance calculation infinite loop and enforce exact cent precision (#3)

- Fix infinite loop in optimalSettlements when rounding makes balances
  non-zero-sum (e.g. $10 split 3 ways = $3.33 each = $9.99)
- Rewrite balance calculation to use integer cents throughout:
  generateSettlementPlan converts to cents and balances any rounding gap,
  greedySettlements operates purely in integer cents with no float tolerance
- Fix receipt computeSplits/computeBreakdown to use cent-based math with
  remainder distribution so splits always sum exactly to the receipt total
- Tighten transaction validation to exact cent equality (no tolerance) in
  both the API (verifyTransactionDetails) and frontend form submission
- Add 5 regression tests for rounding edge cases with zero-tolerance
  verification (every person must have exactly 0¢ remaining)
- Add copilot-instructions.md to .gitignore

fix: allow all CORS request headers to fix payment preflight failure

rs/cors v1.11.1 was rejecting preflight requests containing
Access-Control-Request-Headers despite those headers being listed
in AllowedHeaders. Using wildcard '*' tells the library to echo
back whatever headers the browser requests, fixing the 'Failed to
fetch' error during booking/payment.

commit.message:
Rewrite waveform: proper DC filter + MA filter + inversion + per-frame scaling

Based on the approach used by zuoyi001/oximeter_max30102 and other
proven open-source pulse oximeter projects:

1. DC-removal high-pass filter (alpha=0.95) strips the large constant
   IR baseline, leaving only the pulsatile AC component
2. 4-sample moving average smooths high-frequency sensor noise
3. Signal is INVERTED — the PPG signal drops during a heartbeat (more
   blood = more light absorbed = lower reading), so inverting gives
   the familiar upward-peak pulse shape
4. Per-frame auto-scaling finds min/max of the entire circular buffer
   and maps to the display height every frame — waveform always fills
   the screen regardless of signal amplitude
5. LED power increased from 0x1F (6.4mA) to 0x7F (25.4mA) for a
   stronger signal and more reliable beat detection
6. 50-sample warmup period lets the DC filter settle before drawing
7. Circular buffer with proper read ordering (oldest→newest, left→right)

commit.message:
Replace raw PPG waveform with synthetic ECG triggered by beat detection

The MAX30102 is a PPG sensor — its raw IR signal is a smooth sinusoidal
wave (blood volume changes), not the sharp QRS spikes of an ECG. No
amount of filtering can make it look like a hospital monitor.

New approach: define a 28-sample PQRST waveform template in PROGMEM
with the classic ECG shape (P wave, QRS spike, T wave). checkForBeat()
triggers playback of the template into the scrolling buffer. Between
beats the line stays flat at the baseline.

Removes all DC removal / EMA / AC scaling / calibration warmup code —
none of it is needed since we're no longer displaying the raw signal.

commit.message:
feat: /health endpoint with real diagnostic checks and 503 on failure (#99)

The existing `/api/health` stub returned a hardcoded `{ status:
"healthy" }` with no actual infrastructure checks. This replaces it with
a proper diagnostic endpoint suitable for post-deployment smoke tests
and monitoring.

## Changes

### `api/src/services/health-checks.js` (new)
Four named checks with a `runAllChecks(options)` aggregator:

| Check | What it tests |
|---|---|
| `storage_connection_string` | `AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING` env
var is set |
| `table_storage` | Live `TableServiceClient.listTables()` connectivity
probe |
| `scenarios_table` | Reports active table name (`TABLE_NAME_SCENARIOS`
or `userscenarios`) |
| `profiles_table` | Reports active table name (`TABLE_NAME_PROFILES` or
`userprofiles`) |

`checkTableStorageConnectivity` accepts an injectable `clientFactory`
for unit testing without Azure infrastructure.

### `api/src/functions/health.js` (updated)
Calls `runAllChecks()` and maps result to HTTP status — **200** when all
pass, **503** on any failure.

Response shape:
```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "timestamp": "2026-03-08T00:00:00.000Z",
  "service": "CCS Calculator API",
  "checks": [
    { "name": "storage_connection_string", "status": "ok" },
    { "name": "table_storage",             "status": "ok" },
    { "name": "scenarios_table",           "status": "ok", "detail": "userscenarios" },
    { "name": "profiles_table",            "status": "ok", "detail": "userprofiles"  }
  ]
}
```

### `api/tests/services/health-checks.test.js` (new)
16 unit tests covering all four checks individually and `runAllChecks`
aggregation, using injected mock factories — no Azure emulator required.

### Docs
- `documentation/api-reference.md` — updated health section with full
check table and 200/503 examples
- `master-plan.md` — Phase 10 entry added

commit.message:
Fix continuous wave: switch to unipolar pulse display

The bipolar AC scaling (tracking both acMin and acMax) stretched the
dip between beats all the way to the top of the display, making it look
like a continuous sine wave with no gaps between beats.

Switch to unipolar display matching real pulse oximeters:
- Only track positive peaks (acMax) with slow decay (0.998/sample)
- Map [0, acMax] → [bottom, top]; negative values clamp to bottom
- Result: flat baseline between beats, sharp spike up on each heartbeat

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: include chainId in pool reserves cache key to prevent cross-chain collision

getAssetHubPoolReserves$ cached observables using only the numeric poolAssetId
as the key. Both Kusama and Polkadot Asset Hubs have sequentially numbered pools
(0, 1, 2, ...), so switching networks returned cached reserves from the wrong
chain — producing wildly incorrect token prices on the portfolio page.

Fix: prefix cache key with chainId (e.g. 'pah::5' vs 'kah::5') and rename
the namespace from 'getPoolReserves$' to 'getAssetHubPoolReserves$' to avoid

commit.message:
Fix symmetric triangle waveform: slow EMA alpha 0.05 -> 0.005

With alpha=0.05 the DC baseline had a ~0.4s time constant — shorter than
the ~0.87s beat interval at 69 BPM — so the EMA was tracking the pulse
itself, producing a symmetric up/down triangle with no gaps.

Changing to alpha=0.005 gives a ~4s time constant. The baseline now tracks
only slow drift, leaving the true PPG pulse shape (fast rise, slower fall
with dicrotic notch, flat gap between beats) visible in the AC signal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix startup waveform swing: add calibration warmup phase

The EMA baseline was initializing from a low value during finger placement
then slowly climbing to the real level (~250k), causing a large transient
swing that pushed the line to the bottom before settling.

Fix:
- Separate DC update from waveform drawing
- During warmup (60 samples, ~1.2s): use fast alpha (0.5) to snap the
  baseline to the correct level in ~6 samples; show 'Hold still...' with
  a progress bar instead of drawing the waveform
- After warmup: switch to slow alpha (0.05) for gentle drift tracking;
  draw waveform normally with AC-only signal

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace DAISY Pipeline with liblouis for BRF conversion

DAISY Pipeline 2 always requires --data (TTS config) even for braille
conversion — a design limitation unrelated to actual TTS.

Switch to liblouisutdml (file2brl) which is the braille-only library:
- No TTS needed — it's pure text-to-braille-dots translation
- Simpler: pandoc extracts HTML from EPUB, file2brl produces BRF
- Available as apt package on ubuntu-latest
